classDescription: A precision skirmisher built on stealth, expertise, and timing-sensitive burst damage.
roleIdentity: Skill specialist and precision striker: create openings, trigger Sneak Attack, and outmaneuver threats with cunning setup tools.

rogue-expertise-1.summary: Choose 2 skills or tools you are proficient in and double your proficiency bonus for those checks.
rogue-expertise-1.description: Choose 2 of your skill or tool proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check using one of those proficiencies. At level 6 you choose 2 more proficiencies to apply Expertise to.

rogue-sneak-attack-1d6.summary: Once per turn, deal 1d6 extra damage when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage or a nearby ally.
rogue-sneak-attack-1d6.description: Once per turn, you can deal extra damage to one creature you hit with an attack if you have Advantage on the attack roll, or if at least one of your allies is within 5 feet of the target and that ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition — provided you don't have Disadvantage on the attack roll. The attack must use a Finesse or a ranged weapon. The extra damage starts at 1d6 and increases by 1d6 at every odd-numbered rogue level (2d6 at level 3, 3d6 at level 5, and so on up to 10d6 at level 19).
rogue-sneak-attack-1d6.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or a non-incapacitated ally within 5 feet of the target) with no Disadvantage on your roll, add 1d6 extra damage.

rogue-thieves-cant.summary: You know the secret language and signs of the criminal underworld, and can pass hidden messages through ordinary conversation.
rogue-thieves-cant.description: You know Thieves' Cant, a secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code used by rogues and criminals. You can use it to pass hidden messages to other Thieves' Cant speakers in normal conversation — only another speaker can understand the hidden meaning. It takes four times as long to convey a message this way as to speak plainly.

rogue-cunning-action.summary: Use your Bonus Action to Dash, Disengage, or Hide on every turn.
rogue-cunning-action.description: On each of your turns you can use a Bonus Action to take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action. Dash doubles your movement speed for the turn. Disengage means your movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks. Hide lets you attempt to become Hidden (Stealth check vs. passive Perception).

rogue-roguish-archetype-subclass.summary: Choose your Roguish Archetype subclass, which grants you specialized features at levels 3, 9, 13, and 17.
rogue-roguish-archetype-subclass.description: At level 3 you choose a Roguish Archetype — the criminal specialty you have pursued. This choice is your subclass gate: it defines your build path and unlocks your unique features at level 3, and again at levels 9, 13, and 17.

rogue-steady-aim.summary: Use your Bonus Action to give yourself Advantage on your next attack this turn; your Speed becomes 0 until the end of the turn.
rogue-steady-aim.description: As a Bonus Action, you give yourself Advantage on your next attack roll this turn. Your Speed becomes 0 until the end of the turn, and you can't take reactions until the start of your next turn. You must use this Bonus Action before making the attack roll it affects.

rogue-cunning-strike.summary: When you deal Sneak Attack damage, forgo 1d6 of it to impose a bonus effect on the target.
rogue-cunning-strike.description:
When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can forgo some of those dice to impose one of the following effects on the target. The save DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Dexterity modifier.

- Disarm (costs 1d6): The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or drop one item it is holding (your choice).
- Poison (costs 1d6): The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be Poisoned until the start of your next turn.
- Trip (costs 1d6): The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be knocked Prone. Large or smaller creatures only.
- Withdraw (costs 1d6): Your movement this turn doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks from the target.

rogue-evasion.summary: When an effect lets you make a Dexterity saving throw for half damage, you take no damage on a success and only half on a failure.
rogue-evasion.description: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage on a successful save and only half damage on a failed save. You can't use this feature if you are Incapacitated.

rogue-reliable-talent.summary: When you make an ability check using a skill or tool you are proficient in, treat any d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.
rogue-reliable-talent.description: Whenever you make an ability check that lets you add your proficiency bonus, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10. This applies to any check — skill, tool, or otherwise — where your proficiency bonus is added, including checks where you have Expertise.

rogue-improved-cunning-strike.summary: You can now apply two Cunning Strike effects on the same Sneak Attack, paying both dice costs.
rogue-improved-cunning-strike.description: When you use Cunning Strike, you can now apply two Cunning Strike effects on the same attack, deducting the Sneak Attack dice cost for each effect separately. You must have enough Sneak Attack dice remaining to pay for both effects.

rogue-devious-strikes.summary: Gain three new, more powerful Cunning Strike options: Daze, Knock Out, and Obscure.
rogue-devious-strikes.description:
You gain three new Cunning Strike options. The save DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Dexterity modifier.

- Daze (costs 2d6): The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be Incapacitated until the end of your next turn.
- Knock Out (costs 6d6): The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be Unconscious for 1 minute. The unconscious condition ends early if the target takes any damage or if another creature uses an action to shake the target awake.
- Obscure (costs 3d6): The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be Blinded until the end of your next turn.

rogue-slippery-mind.summary: You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws and Intelligence saving throws.
rogue-slippery-mind.description: Your mind becomes resistant to outside influence. You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws and Intelligence saving throws. If you already have proficiency in one of these saving throws from another source, you gain proficiency in the other one instead.

rogue-subtle-strikes.summary: When an ally is within 5 feet of a creature, you have Advantage on your attacks against that creature even if you have a source of Disadvantage.
rogue-subtle-strikes.description: When you make an attack roll against a creature, you have Advantage on the roll if at least one of your allies is within 5 feet of the creature and that ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition. This Advantage applies even if you would otherwise have Disadvantage on the roll — the two conditions cancel, and you roll with Advantage.

rogue-elusive.summary: No attack roll has Advantage against you while you are not Incapacitated.
rogue-elusive.description: You are so evasive that attackers rarely get the upper hand against you. No attack roll can have Advantage against you while you are not Incapacitated. This benefit applies regardless of how the attacker gained Advantage — spells, features, conditions, or flanking.

rogue-stroke-of-luck.summary: Once per Short or Long Rest, treat a missed attack or failed ability check as though you rolled a 20 on the d20.
rogue-stroke-of-luck.description: If you fail an ability check, you can treat the d20 roll as a 20. If you miss with an attack roll, you can treat the d20 roll as a 20. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short Rest or Long Rest.

rogue-expertise-2.summary: Choose 2 more skills or tools you are proficient in and double your proficiency bonus for those checks.
rogue-expertise-2.description: You gain Expertise in two more skills or tools of your choice from your existing proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check using those selected proficiencies.

rogue-sneak-attack-l3.summary: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 2d6.
rogue-sneak-attack-l3.description: Your Sneak Attack damage is now 2d6. Once per turn, when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon attack and you have Advantage on the roll, or when a non-incapacitated ally is within 5 feet of the target and you don't have Disadvantage, you deal an extra 2d6 damage.
rogue-sneak-attack-l3.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l5.summary: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 3d6.
rogue-sneak-attack-l5.description: Your Sneak Attack damage is now 3d6. Once per turn, when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon attack and you have Advantage on the roll, or when a non-incapacitated ally is within 5 feet of the target and you don't have Disadvantage, you deal an extra 3d6 damage.
rogue-sneak-attack-l5.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l7.summary: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 4d6.
rogue-sneak-attack-l7.description: Your Sneak Attack damage is now 4d6. Once per turn, when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon attack and you have Advantage on the roll, or when a non-incapacitated ally is within 5 feet of the target and you don't have Disadvantage, you deal an extra 4d6 damage.
rogue-sneak-attack-l7.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l9.summary: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 5d6.
rogue-sneak-attack-l9.description: Your Sneak Attack damage is now 5d6. Once per turn, when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon attack and you have Advantage on the roll, or when a non-incapacitated ally is within 5 feet of the target and you don't have Disadvantage, you deal an extra 5d6 damage.
rogue-sneak-attack-l9.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l11.summary: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 6d6.
rogue-sneak-attack-l11.description: Your Sneak Attack damage is now 6d6. Once per turn, when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon attack and you have Advantage on the roll, or when a non-incapacitated ally is within 5 feet of the target and you don't have Disadvantage, you deal an extra 6d6 damage.
rogue-sneak-attack-l11.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l13.summary: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 7d6.
rogue-sneak-attack-l13.description: Your Sneak Attack damage is now 7d6. Once per turn, when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon attack and you have Advantage on the roll, or when a non-incapacitated ally is within 5 feet of the target and you don't have Disadvantage, you deal an extra 7d6 damage.
rogue-sneak-attack-l13.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l15.summary: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 8d6.
rogue-sneak-attack-l15.description: Your Sneak Attack damage is now 8d6. Once per turn, when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon attack and you have Advantage on the roll, or when a non-incapacitated ally is within 5 feet of the target and you don't have Disadvantage, you deal an extra 8d6 damage.
rogue-sneak-attack-l15.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l17.summary: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 9d6.
rogue-sneak-attack-l17.description: Your Sneak Attack damage is now 9d6. Once per turn, when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon attack and you have Advantage on the roll, or when a non-incapacitated ally is within 5 feet of the target and you don't have Disadvantage, you deal an extra 9d6 damage. Your precision with precision strikes and tactical positioning ensures the damage hits where it counts.
rogue-sneak-attack-l17.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l19.summary: Your Sneak Attack damage increases to 10d6.
rogue-sneak-attack-l19.description: Your Sneak Attack damage is now 10d6. Once per turn, when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon attack and you have Advantage on the roll, or when a non-incapacitated ally is within 5 feet of the target and you don't have Disadvantage, you deal an extra 10d6 damage.
rogue-sneak-attack-l19.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l20.summary: Your Sneak Attack damage remains at 10d6 at level 20.
rogue-sneak-attack-l20.description: Your Sneak Attack damage remains 10d6 at level 20. Once per turn, when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon attack and you have Advantage on the roll, or when a non-incapacitated ally is within 5 feet of the target and you don't have Disadvantage, you deal an extra 10d6 damage.
rogue-sneak-attack-l20.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-ability-score-improvement-l4.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each, or gain a feat.
rogue-ability-score-improvement-l4.description: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. Alternatively, if your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat. You can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

rogue-ability-score-improvement-l8.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each, or gain a feat.
rogue-ability-score-improvement-l8.description: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. Alternatively, if your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat. You can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

rogue-ability-score-improvement-l12.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each, or gain a feat.
rogue-ability-score-improvement-l12.description: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. Alternatively, if your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat. You can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

rogue-ability-score-improvement-l16.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each, or gain a feat.
rogue-ability-score-improvement-l16.description: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. Alternatively, if your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat. You can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

rogue-subclass-feature-l10.summary: Your Roguish Archetype grants you an additional feature at level 10.
rogue-subclass-feature-l10.description: Your chosen Roguish Archetype grants you a feature at level 10. The specific feature depends on your subclass. Consult your archetype's description for details.

rogue-subclass-feature-l14.summary: Your Roguish Archetype grants you an additional feature at level 14.
rogue-subclass-feature-l14.description: Your chosen Roguish Archetype grants you a feature at level 14. The specific feature depends on your subclass. Consult your archetype's description for details.

rogue-subclass-feature-l15.summary: Your Roguish Archetype grants you an additional feature at level 15.
rogue-subclass-feature-l15.description: Your chosen Roguish Archetype grants you a feature at level 15. The specific feature depends on your subclass. Consult your archetype's description for details.

rogue-epic-boon-l19.summary: Gain an Epic Boon feat, which also increases one ability score by 1 (up to a maximum of 30).
rogue-epic-boon-l19.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat, or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. If you choose an Epic Boon feat, that feat's ability score increase can raise an ability score above 20, up to a maximum of 30.

progressionSummary.summary: Roguish Archetype (Subclass), Steady Aim, Sneak Attack (2d6).

buildTips.primaryReason: powers attacks, Armor Class, stealth, and initiative
buildTips.secondaryReason: extra HP helps you survive when plans go wrong
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: Perception and common saves use Wisdom more than many new rogues expect
buildTips.playPatternHint: Use Cunning Action or Steady Aim to set up one high-value Sneak Attack each turn; spend Cunning Strike riders when control is worth more than raw damage.
